title: Proxmox Storage Replication (pvesr) Sync Failure and Lag
app: Custom REST poll of `/api2/json/nodes/{node}/replication` or Universal Forwarder on nodes running `pvesr`
spl:
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:replication" earliest=-24h
| eval lag_sec=now()-last_sync_epoch
| where fail_count>0 OR error_state=1 OR lag_sec>3600
| stats max(lag_sec) as max_lag_sec, max(fail_count) as fails by job_id, guest, target_node
| sort -fails, -max_lag_sec
description: Scheduled storage replication between Proxmox nodes protects guests when a site or rack fails. A silent replication stall means the standby copy is stale, invalidating DR assumptions until the next failover test.
value: Keeps RPO honest for pvesr-based DR by exposing replication errors and excessive lag before a real disaster.
implementation: Use a modular input or cron script to call the replication status API per node and HEC the JSON. Normalize `last_sync` to epoch seconds. Alert on non-zero `fail_count`, explicit error flags, or lag beyond the SLA (for example 1 hour for hourly jobs).
visualization: Table (jobs over lag threshold), Timeline (sync success), Single value (jobs in error).
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
